name: Chocolate Glazed Bailey's Irish Cream Cheesec
recipeInstructions:
For crust: Preheat oven to 350°F. Finely grind graham crackers in a food processor. Add butter and blend until combined. Press the crust mixture onto the bottom (not sides) of a 9-inch-diameter spring form pan. Bake the crust until golden brown, about 8 minutes. Transfer the crust to a rack. Maintain the oven temperature.
Filling: Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese, sugar in a large bowl until smooth. Beat in flour. Add eggs one at a time, beating just until combined. Mix in remaining ingredients. Pour the filling into the crust. Place the cheesecake pan on the rack. Bake for 10 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 250°F. Bake the cheesecake until set, about 40 minutes longer. Cool the cake pan on the rack for 10 minutes. Run a sharp knife around the pan sides to loosen. Cool.
Glaze: Bring cream to simmer in a heavy medium saucepan. Reduce heat to low. Add chopped chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Cool glaze to lukewarm. Release the sides of the cheesecake, place on a rack over a baking sheet. Pour glaze over cheesecake and spread with a spatula to cover the top, allowing excess to drip onto the sheet. Refrigerate to allow the glaze to set.
